articles.title: Apple’s new CEO, and why Elon Musk wants to buy Cursor for $60B
articles.snippet: A new era is on the way for Apple as Tim Cook plans to step down from his CEO role in September, handing the reins to hardware chief John Ternus.   Ternus may be inheriting one of the most durable businesses in tech, but he’s also stepping into a very different ecosystem than the one Cook spent decades shaping. The App […]
articles.ai_summary: Tim Cook is set to step down as Apple's CEO in September, passing leadership to hardware chief John Ternus. This transition marks a significant change for Apple as it adapts to a new tech landscape.
articles.why_it_matters: Signals a major leadership shift at one of tech's most influential companies.

articles.title: I'm automating these 5 unexpected home devices - and life has been so much easier
articles.snippet: I automated several everyday gadgets, and now my home runs so much more smoothly.

articles.title: Ubuntu Resolute Raccoon spits out Xorg, but still lets you run X11 apps
articles.snippet: New LTS is here, with more tooling for GPGPU and AI workloads Ubuntu 26.04 "Resolute Raccoon," the latest LTS release from Canonical, arrives with GNOME 50, Linux kernel 7.0, and drops the Xorg option from Ubuntu Desktop while still running X11 applications through Xwayland.…
articles.ai_summary: Ubuntu 26.04 'Resolute Raccoon' has been released, introducing GNOME 50 and Linux kernel 7.0 while removing Xorg support. Users can still run X11 apps via Xwayland, enhancing GPGPU and AI capabilities.
articles.why_it_matters: Reflects ongoing advancements in Linux distributions catering to AI and GPU workloads.
